summary: 0.6.58 makes non-trigger movie joystick use the exact existing 15-second skip action once per stick excursion, because the operator wants the same single-action behavior as the skip button and does not want another scrub-math experiment.
operatorSignals: The operator explicitly wants movie joystick to behave like the existing skip button.
The operator explicitly clarified that this should be a single action, not a held repeat.
Image scrubbing was already back in a good place and should stay untouched.
actionsTaken: Removed the custom non-trigger movie target-step math from the movie joystick branch.
Made non-trigger movie joystick dispatch the exact existing skip forward or skip backward action once per stick excursion.
Left image stepping and trigger-modified video behavior unchanged.
nonGoals: No slideshow work in this slice.
No right-stick volume, gaze, or scene-builder changes in this slice.
No image stepping changes in this slice.
